title: Guerrilla co-founder creates EU game engine rival.
body:
In a move that could potentially shake up the game development landscape, a renowned industry veteran is spearheading an ambitious project to create a European game engine, poised to challenge the dominance of established players like Unreal and Unity. Arjan Brussee, co-founder of Guerrilla Games and former tech director at Epic, is at the helm of this initiative, driven by a vision to provide a homegrown alternative to the US-centric engines that have long been the industry standard. This development has significant implications for the global gaming industry, as it could lead to increased diversity in game development, reduced dependence on foreign technology, and the creation of new opportunities for European game developers.

The current game engine market is largely dominated by two major players: Unreal and Unity. These engines have been the backbone of countless game development projects, offering a wide range of tools and features that cater to the diverse needs of game creators. However, their US-centric origins and focus have led to concerns about the lack of a strong European presence in the market. Brussee's initiative aims to address this gap by creating a game engine that is tailored to the specific needs and requirements of European game developers. This could lead to the development of more region-specific games, as well as increased collaboration and innovation among European game developers.

The creation of a European game engine also raises important questions about the future of game development in the region. For years, European game developers have relied heavily on foreign technology, often with limited input in the development process. A homegrown game engine could change this dynamic, allowing European developers to have a greater say in the tools and features that shape their craft. Moreover, a European game engine could lead to the creation of new job opportunities, as well as increased investment in the region's gaming industry. This, in turn, could have a positive impact on the broader European economy, as the gaming industry continues to grow and evolve.

Brussee's involvement in the project is a significant factor in its potential success. As a seasoned industry veteran with a proven track record, he brings a wealth of experience and expertise to the table. His tenure at Guerrilla Games and Epic has given him a deep understanding of the game development process, as well as the technical requirements of modern game engines. This expertise will be invaluable in shaping the development of the new engine, ensuring that it meets the needs of European game developers and provides a viable alternative to existing solutions.

The road ahead will undoubtedly be challenging, as Brussee and his team face the daunting task of creating a game engine that can compete with established players. However, the potential rewards are significant, and the project has already generated significant interest and excitement within the gaming community. As the project progresses, it will be important to watch how it unfolds, and how it impacts the broader gaming industry. One thing is certain, however: the creation of a European game engine has the potential to be a game-changer, and its impact will be felt for years to come.

As the gaming industry continues to evolve and grow, the need for diverse and innovative solutions has never been more pressing. The development of a European game engine is a significant step in this direction, offering a fresh perspective and a new set of possibilities for game developers. As Brussee and his team work to bring this vision to life, the global gaming community will be watching with great interest, eager to see how this new engine will shape the future of game development. With its potential to increase diversity, innovation, and collaboration, the European game engine is a project that holds great promise, and its success could have far-reaching implications for the gaming industry as a whole.
